page_content:
UNIV362 Designing Your Life after College (2 Credits) Helps students anticipate and prepare for changes in ﬁnances, social life, health, and career dynamics they are likely to experience as they transition from college to working full-time. It focuses on understanding what resources students have as they enter the workforce as critical for navigating post-graduation changes successfully. It offers an overview of ﬁnancial, risk management and professional development approaches to increase students' conﬁdence regarding post-graduation decisions; in addition, it develops their creative problem solving and reflection skills to help them align the outcomes of their decisions with their values. Restriction: Must have earned a minimum of 60 credits.

UNIV218 Study Abroad Exploration (3 Credits) Topics and assignments will vary by travel site. Restriction: Must be in designated Study Abroad programs. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.

UNIV269 Connecting Across Cultures (1 Credit) An examination of culture to guide study abroad students through the stages of cross cultural adjustment and to promote cultural competency by providing the motivation, knowledge and skills necessary to work with diverse individuals and teams. Restriction: Must be in a study abroad program.
